scenario.context: Station 7 of the daily run: costing and profitability.
scenario.mission: You calculate a small workstation package and check the pricing logic.

station.profile: Small procurement with a service component

customerRequest.subject: Costing check Station NS-ST-07
customerRequest.body: Please calculate a small workstation package including a service share and provide a short assessment of price plausibility.

questions.title: Calculate package price
questions.prompt: 4 workstations: per station PC 610 EUR, 2 monitors each 165 EUR, keyboard/mouse 30 EUR, setup 0.8 h at 62 EUR/h. What is the total amount (excl. tax)?

feedback.wrong: Please combine material and time per station, then multiply.

questions.title: Assess economic sense
questions.prompt: Which statement best describes an economically sound decision?

options.rationale: This can create follow-up costs and downtime risks.

options.text: Assess price, suitability, maintainability, and support together
options.rationale: A holistic evaluation reduces wrong decisions.

feedback.correct: Correct: economic efficiency is more than purchase price.
feedback.wrong: Please consider technical suitability and operating costs.

questions.title: Cost components
questions.prompt: Which items typically belong in a workstation calculation? (Multiple selections)

options.rationale: Service share belongs in the overall calculation.
Can be relevant for a realistic overall view.

feedback.correct: Correct: You separate relevant cost items cleanly.
feedback.wrong: Partly off: Please distinguish calculation-relevant items more clearly.

questions.title: Costing workflow
questions.prompt: Put the steps in a sensible order.
items: Calculate material and labor cost per station
Calculate the total for all stations
Note a short justification for the decision
correctOrder: Calculate material and labor cost per station
Calculate the total for all stations
Note a short justification for the decision

feedback.correct: Correct: calculation steps and assessment are structured cleanly.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: start with data, then calculation and plausibility.

questions.title: Price assessment
questions.prompt: Write a short assessment (4-6 sentences) why the calculated price is plausible or implausible.
questions.expectedOutline: Short verdict with cost reference, technical benefit, and justified assessment.

feedback.wrong: Please justify the price verdict more with costs and benefits.
